evals.prompt: 70% of my orders are single-item. What should I cross-sell and where?
evals.expected_output: Identifies the single-item share as the addressable cross-sell surface. Pulls SKU affinity and order-composition mix before recommending specific cross-sells. Walks through the three placement options (in-cart, post-purchase one-click, delayed email) and matches SKU selection rules to each. Should not jump to a single SKU recommendation without pulling affinity first.
assertions: Identifies single-item share as the addressable surface
Calls modern.sales.sku_affinity for affinity matrix
Calls modern.sales.aov grouped by item_count
Walks through in-cart vs post-purchase one-click vs delayed email placements
Names the post-purchase one-click moment as the highest-conversion placement
Recommends different SKU selection rules per placement
Output has Quick Wins, High-Impact, Test Ideas sections

evals.prompt: My top seller is a $58 moisturizer. What should I cross-sell after it?
evals.expected_output: Pulls SKU affinity for the moisturizer specifically, distinguishes co-purchase from sequential affinity, and applies the margin-weighted selection rule. Walks through which SKU goes in-cart vs post-purchase one-click vs delayed email. Discusses cross-sell creative angle and warns against hard discounts on cross-sell SKUs.
assertions: Calls modern.sales.sku_affinity with the moisturizer as the anchor
Distinguishes co-purchase affinity from sequential affinity
Applies margin-weighted vs revenue-weighted selection rule
Maps different SKUs to different placements
Recommends continuity language over acquisition language for cross-sell creative
Warns against hard discounts on cross-sell SKUs
Output has Quick Wins, High-Impact, Test Ideas sections

evals.prompt: Should I send a cross-sell email day 3 after purchase or day 14?
evals.expected_output: Walks through the timing-after-first-purchase windows. Day 3-7 is the receipt-and-impression window suitable only for items that do not depend on first-product validation. Day 7-21 is the usage-and-validation window, the high-conversion window for value-extension cross-sells. Calibrates the window to the first SKU consumption cycle.
assertions: Walks through the timing windows (0-2, 3-7, 7-21, 21-45, 45+)
Names day 7-21 as the high-conversion usage-and-validation window
Calibrates the window to consumption cycle of the first SKU
Distinguishes value-extension cross-sells from gifting/accessory cross-sells
Does not recommend a fixed calendar day without calibration
Output has Quick Wins, High-Impact, Test Ideas sections

evals.prompt: Customers who buy product A often buy product B too. Should I bundle them or cross-sell them?
evals.expected_output: Walks through the cross-sell vs bundle decision tree. Bundles compress the buying decision; cross-sells extend it. Bundles when both have above-baseline solo purchase rates and the discount preserves price floor; cross-sells when one is hero and one is supporting. Discusses cannibalization risk if running both simultaneously.
assertions: Walks through the cross-sell vs bundle decision tree
Names when to bundle (both above baseline, discount preserves floor)
Names when to cross-sell (hero plus supporting, value depends on primary)
Discusses cannibalization risk of running both simultaneously
References bundle-pricing skill for the bundle path
Output has Quick Wins, High-Impact, Test Ideas sections

evals.prompt: Our post-purchase thank you page just says thanks. We're not cross-selling there. Big deal?
evals.expected_output: Identifies the post-purchase one-click placement as the highest-conversion cross-sell moment in ecomm, typically 8-18%. Pulls affinity data to recommend the best margin-per-impression SKU. Warns against surfacing high-consideration items here. Names this as a single highest-leverage move for most brands without it.
assertions: Identifies post-purchase one-click as highest-conversion placement
Cites typical 8-18% conversion band
Pulls modern.sales.sku_affinity to identify the right SKU
Recommends the highest-margin affinity candidate
Names this as a single highest-leverage move
Warns against surfacing high-consideration items in this slot
Output has Quick Wins, High-Impact, Test Ideas sections

evals.prompt: We sell one-time. Can a cross-sell turn the customer into a subscriber?
evals.expected_output: Identifies the subscription cross-sell as the highest-LTV cross-sell move in ecomm. Recommends placing the subscription commitment in the post-purchase one-click slot when the original was one-time. Connects the move to LTV economics and references paid-ltv-optimization for the deeper analysis. Pulls repeat rate to identify which first-SKUs are best subscription cross-sell candidates.
assertions: Names subscription cross-sell as highest-LTV cross-sell move
Recommends post-purchase one-click placement for the subscription offer
Calls modern.retention.repeat_rate grouped by first_sku
Connects to LTV economics
Distinguishes subscription-friendly first SKUs from one-and-done first SKUs
Output has Quick Wins, High-Impact, Test Ideas sections

evals.prompt: Our cross-sell on the cart page has 6 different options. Is that too many?
evals.expected_output: Identifies the decision cost of comparison as a cross-sell killer. Pre-curating to 1-2 options per primary SKU outperforms 6-option presentations. Walks through reduced-friction copy, single image, single CTA template, and explains why cross-sell creative differs from main-line creative.
assertions: Identifies 6 options as too many
Recommends pre-curating to 1-2 options per primary SKU
Explains decision cost of comparison as a cross-sell killer
Recommends short copy, single image, single CTA template
Distinguishes cross-sell creative from main-line creative
Output has Quick Wins, High-Impact, Test Ideas sections

evals.prompt: Our cross-sell post-purchase flow is generating $0.22 per recipient. Is that good?
evals.expected_output: Identifies $0.22 as below the typical band (under $0.40 per recipient is underperforming). Pulls flow performance and affinity data to diagnose. Likely causes: wrong SKU selected for placement, hard discount eroding margin, too many options, or creative tuned to acquisition rather than continuity. Walks through specific diagnostic checks before recommending fixes.
assertions: Identifies $0.22 as below the typical $0.40+ band
Calls modern.flows.revenue_by_flow filtered to post-purchase
Lists likely causes (wrong SKU, hard discount, too many options, wrong creative)
Walks diagnostic before recommending fixes
Output has Quick Wins, High-Impact, Test Ideas sections
